page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The instance is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the e2-highcpu-4 delivers modest performance. Single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, generally fall into the weak category, representing the bottom 25% of tested servers. However, the instance shows average tier performance in specific workloads, such as single-threaded gzip compression at 25.69 GB/sec, single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 10.75 GB/sec, and Geekbench text processing. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes also perform in the average range, though small-block cached reads and overall Passmark memory scores are poor, landing in the bottom 10%.

Given its low memory allocation relative to compute cores, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for CPU-bound tasks that do not demand a large memory footprint. It is suited for lightweight batch processing, basic web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and small-scale software compilation. However, the 4.0 GB memory limitation and weak database operation benchmarks make it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor. Featuring a dedicated CPU allocation with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks position the server primarily in the lower tiers, with weak single-core and multi-core CPU scores, alongside poor small-block memory bandwidth. However, it achieves average-tier results in large-block memory operations, single-threaded gzip compression, and text processing. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, the e2-highcpu-4 is a budget-friendly option designed for lightweight, CPU-focused workloads such as basic web serving, software compilation, and simple batch processing tasks that do not require substantial memory resources.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor, designed for budget-friendly, low-memory compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU, optimized for low-memory tasks.
tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-skewed virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a compact memory footprint for lightweight processing.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
Configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers average performance in single-threaded compression and text processing
Features a dedicated CPU allocation model with x86_64 architecture
Best suited for lightweight batch processing, basic web serving, and simple compilation tasks
